issues.detail: The fixtures are unresolvable bindings, not spoofed attribution. The statement that falsely attributed burn "never lands on any task" contradicts the requirement's explicit classification as spoofable: a resolvable but dishonest same-project task binding can be recorded against that task.
issues.fix: Rename these fixtures unresolvable/invalid and remove the global false-attribution guarantee. Add a resolvable-but-wrong same-project binding fixture showing that telemetry remains spoofable and non-gating.

issues.detail: "Every metered AI request" and the required "provider status" field imply coverage when the provider returns a non-success status. All listed fixtures are ordinary turns, so logging of provider failures/rejections is untested.
issues.fix: Add controlled non-success provider responses on gateway and direct handlers; assert each still produces exactly one row with the returned provider status and echoed usage data supplied by the fixture.

issues.detail: Reading the known log afterward cannot prove that it was written in-line by the handling component or that it is the only usage log. An asynchronous queue or second shadow log could pass every current assertion.
issues.fix: Pause background workers/event delivery and assert the row exists before the request handler returns, instrument the writer identity, and add an absence probe for any second usage store or usage-event publication.

issues.detail: Its unqualified claim that fuel is "never from token math" conflicts with REQ-006/REQ-009, which require the live fuel gauge to price the provider's per-workspace usage report using the provider's official rate card. The intended prohibition is using per-request attribution-row tokens as money truth.
issues.fix: Scope the assertion to "never derived from attribution-log rows or per-request attribution token math" while permitting the PRD-required provider-report pricing path.

issues.detail: Holding provider and Lovable snapshots constant only proves that attribution-row writes do not move the meters. A permanently constant or unrelated meter also passes, so "rendered from provider truth alone" is not established.
issues.fix: With attribution rows fixed, change each authoritative provider snapshot and Lovable truth independently and assert the corresponding meter/status changes to the expected value, or cite owning P0 tests that establish those positive mappings.

issues.detail: No test exercises the clause that "the agent is nudged to know its task context and select the matching task when exploration becomes implementation." AT-034.12 only labels steering conversational and verifies non-gating plus Skill disablement.
issues.fix: Start in exploration, introduce implementation intent, and assert that a non-blocking matching-task selection nudge is shown; also assert forwarding remains available if the suggestion is not followed.

issues.detail: Both tests assert that per-volunteer-per-task detail is absent from NGO surfaces. The round-1 expansion of AT-034.14 duplicated the NGO-side assertion already present in AT-034.13.
issues.fix: Keep NGO aggregation assertions in AT-034.13; let AT-034.14 test coordinator access plus absence from public/non-coordinator surfaces without repeating the NGO assertion.
